userMenu.walletNotConnected: Connect a wallet to fund deals and receive payouts.

home.badge: Built on Stellar · Trustless Work escrow
home.description: PyMEs get working capital, investors earn 8-15% APR on USDC deals, and suppliers receive guaranteed milestone payments - all secured by non-custodial escrow on Stellar.
home.cycleSubtitle: Four roles, one transparent loop — secured by Stellar escrow at every step.
home.cycleRepeats: Cycle repeats with the next deal
home.cycleStep1Body: Selects a supplier, sets the order amount, defines payment milestones, and deploys a non-custodial escrow on Stellar.
home.cycleStep1BodyDesktop: Picks a supplier, sets the order amount, defines payment milestones, and deploys a non-custodial escrow on Stellar.
home.cycleStep2Body: Browses open deals, chooses one, and sends USDC directly into the smart contract. Funds are locked until milestones are approved.
home.cycleStep3Body: Ships goods and uploads delivery proof on-chain. Escrow releases USDC in milestones upon PyME approval.
home.cycleStep3BodyMobile: Ships the goods and uploads delivery proof on-chain. Escrow releases USDC in milestones upon PyME approval.
home.cycleStep4Body: After selling the inventory, the PyME repays principal plus yield (8–15% APR). The cycle completes transparently on-chain.
home.cycleStep4BodyMobile: After selling the inventory, the PyME repays principal plus yield (8–15% APR). The cycle completes on-chain.
home.trustSubtitle: Non-custodial, on-chain, and auditable — every step of the deal cycle.
home.trustCard1Body: Funds go directly into a Stellar smart contract via Trustless Work. MERCATO never touches investor capital — only the contract logic authorizes a release.
home.trustCard2Body: Suppliers receive USDC in stages as milestones are approved — no waiting on net-30/60 terms. Investors get repaid at the end of the deal term with full yield.
home.trustCard3Body: Every escrow deployment, funding, and milestone release requires a signature from your Stellar wallet (Freighter or Albedo). You stay in control at every step.
home.trustCard4Body: Every payment, milestone approval, and delivery confirmation is recorded on Stellar — verifiable by anyone, at any time, without trusting MERCATO.
home.trustCard5Body: All deals are denominated in USDC on Stellar — a stable, dollar-pegged asset. Investors earn 8–15% APR with no crypto-volatility exposure.
home.trustCard6Body: Convert between local currency and USDC within the app via Etherfuse (Mexico, SPEI), Alfred Pay (LATAM), or BlindPay (global) — no third-party exchange needed.
home.footerCopyright: © 2026 MERCATO. Supply Chain Finance on Stellar.

hero.title: Built for Latin American trade, not Wall Street spreadsheets.
hero.subtitle: Mercato connects PyMEs, investors, and suppliers through transparent milestone escrow — so working capital moves at the speed of real supply chains.

mission.quote: Tens of millions of small businesses in Latin America cannot access short-term credit from banks. Mercato exists to close that gap — without adding another opaque intermediary.
mission.statValue: Working capital stuck between buyer, supplier, and investor

what.title: What is Mercato?
what.body: Mercato is a supply chain finance platform that helps small and medium businesses (PyMEs) access working capital through blockchain-secured escrow. Investors fund deals, suppliers receive milestone payments, and every transaction settles in USDC on Stellar — transparently and without a bank in the middle.

why.title: Why we built it
why.body: Without credit, PyMEs cannot pay suppliers upfront. Suppliers wait 60–90 days. Buyers miss sales cycles. Growth stalls. We built Mercato to break that cycle by connecting PyMEs directly with investors through rules-based escrow everyone can verify.

roles.title: Who Mercato serves
roles.subtitle: Three sides of the same purchase order — each with a clear job in the flow.

smb.label: PyMEs (buyers)
smb.description: Short-term capital to pay suppliers while waiting for their own sales to close.

supplier.label: Suppliers
supplier.description: Partial payment at shipment and delivery instead of waiting months for invoices.

investor.label: Investors
investor.description: Transparent, deal-specific returns backed by real trade — not abstract market bets.

flow.title: How Mercato works
flow.subtitle: A milestone-based cycle secured by non-custodial smart contracts on Stellar.

steps.title: Deal setup
steps.description: The PyME describes the purchase, sets the term, and splits supplier payment into milestones.

steps.title: Escrow deployment
steps.description: A non-custodial escrow is deployed via Trustless Work. The PyME signs with their wallet.

steps.title: Investor funding
steps.description: Investors commit USDC into the escrow from the marketplace. Funds stay locked until milestones release.

steps.title: Delivery & releases
steps.description: As the supplier delivers, the PyME approves milestones and the contract pays the supplier on-chain.

steps.title: Repayment
steps.description: At term end, the PyME repays investors their principal and agreed yield.

trust.title: Why blockchain escrow?
trust.body: Fund movement is transparent, auditable, and rule-bound. Neither Mercato nor any single party can move money outside the contract logic. Every release is triggered by milestone approval and settled in seconds.

items.question: Is Mercato a bank?
items.answer: No. Mercato is a platform that coordinates deals. Funds sit in smart escrow contracts, not in a Mercato bank account.

items.question: Who can use Mercato?
items.answer: PyMEs creating purchase orders, suppliers fulfilling them, and investors funding escrows — primarily across Latin American supply chains.

items.question: What currency is used?
items.answer: Deals settle in USDC on Stellar, a dollar-pegged stablecoin, reducing FX friction in regional trade.

hero.title: How MERCATO Works
hero.description: MERCATO connects SMBs, investors, and suppliers through non-custodial, milestone-based escrow on Stellar — giving businesses working capital, investors short-term yield, and suppliers faster payments.

howItWorks.rolesHeading: Who participates in the Mercato ecosystem?
howItWorks.rolesSub: Mercato connects small businesses, investors, and suppliers into a unified, transparent supply chain financing ecosystem. SMBs (buyers) secure essential working capital, investors earn attractive yields by funding real invoice-backed transactions, and suppliers receive guaranteed milestone-based payments on-chain, creating a mutually beneficial loop where everyone wins.

smb.label: SMB (Buyer)
smb.tagline: Get working capital for inventory without taking on traditional debt.
actions: Create deals specifying product, supplier, and payment terms
Deploy a non-custodial Stellar escrow with your wallet
Confirm deliveries and milestone releases
Repay investor capital + yield after your sales cycle

investor.label: Investor
investor.tagline: Fund real supply-chain deals and earn short-term yield in USDC.
actions: Browse open deals on the marketplace
Fund escrow directly from your Stellar wallet in USDC
Track milestones and deal progress in real time
Receive repayment with yield once the deal completes

supplier.label: Supplier
supplier.tagline: Get paid faster with milestone-based on-chain payments.
actions: Manage your company profile and product catalog
Accept orders once a deal is funded
Submit shipment and delivery proof
Receive 50% on shipment and 50% on delivery — on-chain

howItWorks.flowTitle: How does the Mercato deal flow work step-by-step?
howItWorks.flowSub: The Mercato deal lifecycle consists of four key phases secured entirely by smart contracts: a buyer deploys an escrow, an investor funds it with USDC, the supplier ships goods to trigger milestone-based releases, and the buyer finally repays the investor with yield. This automated flow ensures complete security and transparency.

phases.title: SMB Creates Deal & Deploys Escrow
phases.description: An SMB selects a supplier from the catalog, specifies the product, amount, and term, then signs a Stellar wallet transaction to deploy a non-custodial multi-release escrow contract via Trustless Work.
actions: Choose supplier & product from catalog
Set deal amount, term, and APR
Sign escrow deployment with Freighter or Albedo
Deal appears on marketplace as "Seeking Funding"

phases.title: Investor Funds the Escrow
phases.description: An investor browses the open marketplace, reviews the deal terms, APR, and SMB reputation, then sends USDC directly to the escrow contract from their Stellar wallet. Funds never touch the platform.
actions: Browse marketplace and pick a deal
Review APR, term, and SMB track record
Sign transaction to send USDC to escrow
Deal status updates to "Funded" — supplier notified

phases.title: Supplier Ships & Milestones Release
phases.description: The supplier accepts the order, submits shipment proof (Milestone 1), and later delivery confirmation (Milestone 2). Each milestone approval by the SMB triggers an on-chain fund release via Trustless Work.
actions: Supplier submits shipment proof (Milestone 1)
SMB confirms shipment → 50% released to supplier
Supplier submits delivery proof (Milestone 2)
Admin approves → final 50% released on-chain

phases.title: SMB Repays Investor — Deal Complete
phases.description: After the SMB sells the goods and the term completes, they repay the investor the principal plus the agreed yield. The deal is marked "Completed" on-chain, building the SMB's reputation for future financing.
actions: SMB repays principal + yield on Stellar
Transaction hash recorded for transparency
Investor receives USDC + yield in their wallet
Deal marked "Completed" — SMB reputation updated

howItWorks.whyTitle: Why choose Mercato for supply chain finance?
howItWorks.whySub: Mercato is designed specifically to solve Latin America's SME financing gap by leveraging non-custodial blockchain technology. By removing intermediary banks, we offer complete transaction visibility, robust reputation tracking based on repayment history, and integrated fiat-to-USDC on/off-ramp options that simplify access to international decentralized finance markets.

benefits.title: Non-Custodial
benefits.body: Funds go directly into a Stellar smart contract. MERCATO never holds your money — only the contract logic controls release.

benefits.title: Fully Transparent
benefits.body: Every transaction, milestone approval, and proof hash is recorded on-chain. Anyone can verify deal progress at any time.

benefits.title: Reputation-Backed
benefits.body: Each completed deal builds the SMB's on-chain track record. Investors can assess repayment history before funding.

benefits.title: LATAM-Focused
benefits.body: Built for LATAM supply chains with fiat ramps via Etherfuse, Alfred Pay, and BlindPay to convert between USDC and local currency.

howItWorks.poweredBy: What technology powers the Mercato platform?
howItWorks.poweredBySub: Mercato is built on a modern, highly secure decentralized stack designed for web3 accessibility. We leverage the Stellar network for lightning-fast, low-cost token transactions, Trustless Work for managing milestone-gated smart escrow contracts, Freighter and Albedo for non-custodial wallet signatures, and Supabase to host fast, responsive application metadata.
howItWorks.fiatTitle: How does Mercato handle currency conversion between local fiat and USDC?
howItWorks.fiatDescription: Mercato integrates seamlessly with leading digital ramp providers, enabling users to convert local fiat currencies directly to USDC on Stellar. Through SPEI bank transfers via Etherfuse or Alfred Pay, and international integrations via BlindPay, users can deposit capital or cash out earnings instantly without needing third-party crypto exchange accounts.
howItWorks.ctaTitle: How can you get started with Mercato today?
howItWorks.ctaDescription: Getting started with Mercato is a straightforward, three-step process designed for instant onboarding. You can create an account, select your role as a buyer, investor, or supplier, connect a Stellar-compatible wallet, and immediately begin listing deals, funding transactions, or uploading delivery proofs in a secure, digital-first environment.

hero.description: We connect SMEs, suppliers, and investors to finance real orders with secure, transparent payments at every stage.

liveDeal.centerTagline: We mediate so agreements are honored.

sme.label: SMEs
sme.headline: Stop waiting on capital.
sme.body: Create a purchase order, choose a verified supplier, set milestones — then open it to investors. Working capital in hours, not bank cycles.

investor.headline: Yield on real trade.
investor.body: Browse open purchase orders, fund escrow on Stellar, and earn 8–15% APR while production and delivery milestones protect your capital.

supplier.label: Suppliers

supplier.body: USDC is locked before you produce. Payments release when shipment and delivery are confirmed — no chasing invoices across borders.

roles.footerLine: One purchase order links all three — pick your role and enter the flow.

rates.description: SMEs face 19–21% CAT at banks they often cannot access — or 60%+ from informal lenders. Mercato sits in the middle: {apr} on the full purchase order, validated by the deal itself — not your credit file.
rates.stat1Label: of SMEs in LATAM lack access to formal bank credit
rates.stat2Label: of the PO financed on Mercato — not 80% like factoring
rates.stat3Label: APR range — comparable to banks, without their barriers
rates.closingTitle: Mercato isn't cheaper than an informal lender — it's as {accessible} as one, priced closer to a {bank}, and built around the purchase order itself.
rates.closingDisclaimer: Rates shown reflect verified public data (banks, business cards, factoring platforms) and Mercato's target model. Individual deal APR depends on risk, term, and milestones.

mercato.label: Mercato
mercato.subtitle: PO-backed escrow on Stellar
mercato.requirements: Live PO + verified supplier — no banking history

bank.label: Commercial bank (with history)
bank.subtitle: Tier-1 & regional banks
bank.requirements: 2+ years operating, revenue floors, bureau record, collateral
bank.caveat: ~15% of SMEs qualify for formal bank credit

fintech.label: Fintech factoring
fintech.subtitle: e.g. Xepelin
fintech.caveat: Typically advances only ~80% of invoice value

informal.label: Informal lenders
informal.subtitle: Unregulated market
informal.caveat: No protections; effective cost compounds fast

flow.footnote: Illustrative step · milestone escrow on Stellar

order.title: SME creates the order
order.body: Pick a verified supplier, define shipment & delivery milestones, and publish the purchase order to the marketplace.

fund.title: Investor funds it
fund.body: USDC locks in milestone escrow on Stellar. Capital stays protected until each release condition is met on-chain.

produce.title: Supplier produces
produce.body: Production begins with payment secured. The first milestone can release when manufacturing or shipment starts.

deliver.title: Goods deliver
deliver.body: Delivery is confirmed with proof. Final milestone releases remaining funds to the supplier automatically.

complete.title: Everyone wins
complete.body: The SME repays principal plus yield from sales. Investors earn, suppliers were paid, and the cycle can repeat.

faq.description: How funding works, who participates, and what keeps capital safe on Mercato.

whatIsMercato.a: Mercato is a supply chain finance marketplace for Latin America. SMEs post real purchase orders, investors fund them through milestone escrow on Stellar, and suppliers get paid as goods move — all tied to one transparent deal.

whoCanUse.a: SMEs (buyers) create and manage purchase orders. Investors browse open deals and fund escrow. Verified suppliers fulfill orders and receive milestone payments. Each role has a dedicated flow after signing up.

howFunded.a: Once an SME publishes a PO, investors commit USDC into a non-custodial escrow contract. Capital stays locked until predefined milestones — such as production start or delivery — are confirmed on-chain.

milestones.a: Milestones split the deal into stages (for example 50% on shipment, 50% on delivery). Funds release to the supplier only when the agreed proof is submitted and approved, reducing risk for every party.

investorRisk.a: Your funds are held in escrow and tied to a specific purchase order — not a generic credit line. You see the product, supplier, terms, and APR upfront, and releases are gated by milestones rather than discretionary payouts.

supplierPayment.a: After the deal is funded, payments release at each confirmed milestone. Suppliers know funds are secured before production starts, instead of waiting on long invoice cycles after delivery.

stellar.a: Stellar enables fast, low-cost USDC settlement and transparent on-chain escrow. Mercato integrates with the Stellar ecosystem — including partners like Trustless Work — for milestone-based trade finance.

getStarted.a: Create a free account, choose your role (SME, investor, or supplier), and follow the guided flows. SMEs can create a deal in minutes; investors can browse open deals immediately after signing up.

footer.tagline: Supply chain finance for Latin America — purchase orders, milestone escrow, and real-economy returns on Stellar.
